heading: Resolution of clearing banks
label: 339a Resolution of clearing banks
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), was repealed by , , .Section 4422 of this titlePub. L. 111–203, title VII, § 740124 Stat. 1729July 21, 2010
CodificationSection was enacted as section 9B of act , and not as part of section 9 of such act which comprises this subchapter.Dec. 13, 1913
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), was repealed by , , .Section 4422 of this titlePub. L. 111–203, title VII, § 740124 Stat. 1729July 21, 2010

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Board may appoint a conservator or receiver to take possession and control of any uninsured State member bank which operates, or operates as, a multilateral clearing organization pursuant to section 4422 of this title to the same extent and in the same manner as the Comptroller of the Currency may appoint a conservator or receiver for a national bank.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Powers
children.label: 2 Powers
children.text: The conservator or receiver for an uninsured State member bank referred to in paragraph (1) shall exercise the same powers, functions, and duties, subject to the same limitations, as a conservator or receiver for a national bank.

children.heading: Board authority
children.label: b Board authority
children.text: The Board shall have the same authority with respect to any conservator or receiver appointed under subsection (a), and the uninsured State member bank for which the conservator or receiver has been appointed, as the Comptroller of the Currency has with respect to a conservator or receiver for a national bank and the national bank for which the conservator or receiver has been appointed.

children.heading: Bankruptcy proceedings
children.label: c Bankruptcy proceedings
children.text: The Board (in the case of an uninsured State member bank which operates, or operates as, such a multilateral clearing organization) may direct a conservator or receiver appointed for the bank to file a petition pursuant to title 11, in which case, title 11 shall apply to the bank in lieu of otherwise applicable Federal or State insolvency law.
